B The seller may share the code with a third party.
C The seller must hire the buyer's staff.
2. What may the buyer do with the software?
A Share the code with a third party.
B Use it for internal projects.
C Deliver it by August.
3. What must both sides refrain from?
A Notifying each other of a change.
B Performing their duties in good faith.
C Hiring each other's staff for one year.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Modal Verbs of Obligation

GRAMMAR

Para dizer O QUE CADA PARTE TEM DE FAZER num contrato, use um modal verb. Must = deve (obrigação). Shall = deverá (um dever legal formal, muito comum em contratos). May = pode (permissão). Must not = não pode (proibição). Depois do modal vem o verbo na FORMA BASE: "the seller SHALL DELIVER" (não "shall to deliver", não "shall delivers"). / To say WHAT EACH PARTY HAS TO DO in a contract, use a modal verb. Must = obligation. Shall = a formal legal duty (very common in contracts). May = permission. Must not = prohibition. After the modal, use the base verb: "the seller shall deliver" (not "shall to deliver", not "shall delivers").

Word / PalavraMeaning / SentidoExample / Exemplo
must / deveobligation"Each party must notify the other."
shall / deveráa formal legal duty"The seller shall deliver the software."
may / podepermission"The buyer may use it for internal projects."
must not / não podeprohibition"The partner must not share the code."

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"The seller deliver the software by August."
"Each party notify the other before a change."
"The buyer use the software for internal projects."
"The contract the buyer a license."
"The contract sharing the code."
"Both sides must from hiring each other's staff."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?She marks the line that obliges the company to indemnify the partner for any delay, because she thinks it is too heavy.
?Daniel sends the new section about the duties and asks Simone to read it.
?At the call, Simone proposes softer duties to the other party.
?Simone reads that the seller shall deliver the software and that each party must notify the other before a change.
?She notes that the buyer may use the software only for internal projects, and that both sides must refrain from hiring each other's staff.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

The seller shall deliver the software by August.
O vendedor deverá entregar o software até agosto.
Each party must notify the other before a change.
Cada parte deve notificar a outra antes de uma mudança.
The buyer may use the software for internal projects.
O comprador pode usar o software para projetos internos.
The partner must not share the code with a third party.
O parceiro não pode compartilhar o código com um terceiro.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the correct sentence for each situation.

You want to say the seller has the legal duty to deliver by August. What do you say?
A "The seller shall to deliver the software by August."
B "The seller shall deliver the software by August."
C "The seller shall delivers the software by August."
You want to say the partner is not allowed to share the code. How do you say it?
A "The partner must not share the code."
B "The partner do not must share the code."
C "The partner may not to share the code."
You want to give the buyer permission to use the software internally. How do you say it?
A "The buyer must use the software for internal projects."
B "The buyer may use the software for internal projects."
C "The buyer may to use the software for internal projects."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are reviewing the duties of a contract at work. Record yourself describing four duties, using must, shall, may and must not: what the seller shall deliver, what each party must notify, what the buyer may do, and what the partner must not do.

Survival Card -- Lesson 13

1The seller shall deliver the software by August.O vendedor deverá entregar o software até agosto.
2Each party must notify the other before a change.Cada parte deve notificar a outra antes de uma mudança.
3The buyer may use the software for internal projects.O comprador pode usar o software para projetos internos.
4The partner must not share the code with a third party.O parceiro não pode compartilhar o código com um terceiro.
5Both sides must refrain from hiring each other's staff.Ambos os lados devem abster-se de contratar a equipe um do outro.
Aula 14 -- Pre-class

Resolving Disputes -- Arbitration & the First Conditional

Learn the language of disputes and how to resolve them: mediation, arbitration, and a binding award. New vocabulary: dispute, mediation, arbitration, claim, settle, award, binding, resolve. Grammar: the first conditional -- if + present, will + verb ("If mediation fails, we will go to arbitration").
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Dispute -- a disagreement between the parties about the contract
"There is a dispute about the last payment." (Disputa / litígio)
Mediation -- a neutral person helps the parties agree, without deciding
"They will try mediation first." (Mediação)
Arbitration -- a private judge decides the dispute outside court
"If mediation fails, we go to arbitration." (Arbitragem)
Claim -- a formal demand that one party makes against the other
"The partner has a claim for late delivery." (Reivindicação / pedido)
Settle -- to end a dispute by agreement, often before a decision
"If we settle the claim early, we save money." (Resolver por acordo)
Award -- the decision the arbitrator gives at the end of arbitration
"The award is final for both parties." (Sentença arbitral / laudo)
Binding -- a decision that both parties must obey
"The arbitration award is binding." (Vinculante / obrigatório)
Resolve -- to solve a dispute and bring it to an end
"We want to resolve the dispute quickly." (Resolver / solucionar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Dispute
Mediation
Arbitration
Claim
Settle
Award
Binding
Resolve

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

There is a dispute with the German partner about the last payment. The dispute clause says that if the parties cannot agree, they will try mediation first. If they settle the claim in mediation, the dispute ends. But if mediation fails within sixty days, they will go to arbitration in London. If the arbitrator decides, the award will be final and binding on both parties. Simone wants to resolve the dispute quickly, because mediation will save time and money.

1. What will the parties do if they cannot agree?
A They will try mediation first.
B They will go straight to court.
C They will cancel the contract.
2. When will the parties go to arbitration?
A If they settle the claim in mediation.
B If mediation fails within sixty days.
C If the partner pays the last invoice.
3. What will the award be if the arbitrator decides?
A Optional for both parties.
B Final and binding on both parties.
C Valid only for the German partner.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The First Conditional

GRAMMAR

Para falar de um resultado REAL no futuro, use o first conditional. Na condição, use if + presente ("if mediation fails"). No resultado, use will + verbo ("we will go to arbitration"). As duas partes podem trocar de ordem; quando o if vem primeiro, use vírgula. NÃO use will depois de if. / To talk about a real future result, use the first conditional. In the condition use if + present ("if mediation fails"). In the result use will + verb ("we will go to arbitration"). The two parts can swap order; use a comma when if comes first. Do not use will after if.

Part / ParteForm / FormaExample / Exemplo
condition / condiçãoif + present"if mediation fails"
result / resultadowill + verb"we will go to arbitration"
full / completaif + present, will + verb"If mediation fails, we will go to arbitration."

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"There is a about the last payment."
"If the parties cannot agree, they will try ."
"If mediation fails, we will go to ."
"If we the claim early, we will save money."
"If the arbitrator decides, the award will be ."
"We want to the dispute quickly."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?She suggests they settle the claim early to save time and money.
?Daniel tells Simone there is a dispute with the German partner about the last payment.
?The arbitrator gives a binding award, and both sides accept the decision.
?Simone reads the dispute clause and sees that the parties will try mediation first.
?The partner refuses to talk, so the parties start arbitration in London.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

If the parties cannot agree, we will try mediation.
Se as partes não chegarem a acordo, tentaremos a mediação.
If mediation fails, we will go to arbitration.
Se a mediação falhar, iremos para a arbitragem.
If the arbitrator decides, the award will be binding.
Se o árbitro decidir, a sentença será vinculante.
We want to resolve the dispute quickly.
Queremos resolver a disputa rapidamente.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the correct sentence for each situation.

You want to say you will try mediation if the parties cannot agree. What do you say?
A "If the parties will not agree, we try mediation."
B "If the parties cannot agree, we will try mediation."
C "If the parties will agree, we try mediation."
You want to say you will save money if you settle the claim early. How do you say it?
A "If we settle the claim early, we will save money."
B "If we will settle the claim early, we save money."
C "We settle the claim early if we will save money."
You want to say the award will be binding if the arbitrator decides. How do you say it?
A "If the arbitrator will decide, the award is binding."
B "The award will be binding if the arbitrator will decide."
C "If the arbitrator decides, the award will be binding."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine there is a dispute over a contract at work. Record yourself explaining four steps with the first conditional: what you will do if the parties cannot agree, what happens if mediation fails, what the arbitrator gives, and what you will do if you want to settle early.

Survival Card -- Lesson 14

1If the parties cannot agree, we will try mediation.Se as partes não chegarem a acordo, tentaremos a mediação.
2If mediation fails, we will go to arbitration.Se a mediação falhar, iremos para a arbitragem.
3We will save money if we settle the claim early.Economizaremos dinheiro se resolvermos a reivindicação por acordo cedo.
4If the arbitrator decides, the award will be binding.Se o árbitro decidir, a sentença será vinculante.
5We want to resolve the dispute quickly.Queremos resolver a disputa rapidamente.
Aula 15 -- Pre-class

Reading Contracts IV -- Drafting & the Passive Voice

Learn the language lawyers use when they draft and read a contract, and the passive voice of legal English: the agreement is signed, the goods are delivered, the schedules are incorporated. New vocabulary: execute, incorporate, stipulate, deem, render, supersede, annex, construe. Grammar: the passive voice -- be + past participle.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Execute -- to sign a contract and make it legally official
"The contract is executed by both parties." (Assinar / formalizar)
Incorporate -- to include a document so it becomes part of the contract
"The schedules are incorporated into the agreement." (Incorporar)
Stipulate -- to state a condition or term clearly in the contract
"The payment terms are stipulated in clause eight." (Estipular)
Deem -- to treat something as true or accepted under the contract
"The change is deemed accepted when both parties sign." (Considerar / reputar)
Render -- to cause something to become a certain state
"A breach can render the clause invalid." (Tornar)
Supersede -- to replace and cancel an earlier document or term
"This agreement supersedes the old letter of intent." (Substituir / revogar)
Annex -- an extra document attached at the end of a contract
"The price annex is attached to the agreement." (Anexo)
Construe -- to interpret a word or clause and give it a meaning
"The word Goods is construed as the products delivered." (Interpretar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Execute
Incorporate
Stipulate
Deem
Render
Supersede
Annex
Construe

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

The final draft of the service agreement is ready. In the definitions, the word Goods is construed as the products that are delivered under the contract. All the schedules and the price annex are incorporated into the agreement, so they are read as one document. The payment terms are stipulated in the annex. The old letter of intent is superseded by this agreement. The contract is executed when it is signed by both parties.

1. How is the word "Goods" construed?
A As the products that are delivered under the contract.
B As the price stated in the annex.
C As the signature of the parties.
2. What happens to the old letter of intent?
A It is incorporated into the annex.
B It is superseded by this agreement.
C It is signed by one party only.
3. When is the contract executed?
A When the annex is removed.
B When it is signed by both parties.
C When the schedules are deleted.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Passive Voice

GRAMMAR

Contratos usam muito a voz passiva, porque o foco é a COISA, não a pessoa. Forma: be (is / are) + particípio passado ("is signed", "are delivered"). Para dizer quem faz a ação, use by ("is signed by both parties"). Não esqueça o verbo be e use o particípio passado, não o verbo base. / Contracts use the passive voice a lot, because the focus is the THING, not the person. Form: be (is / are) + past participle ("is signed", "are delivered"). To say who does the action, use by ("is signed by both parties"). Do not drop the verb be, and use the past participle, not the base verb.

Part / ParteForm / FormaExample / Exemplo
passive / passivabe + past participle"the agreement is signed"
plural / pluralare + past participle"the goods are delivered"
who / quem (by)be + past participle + by"is signed by both parties"

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"The contract is when it is signed by both parties."
"The schedules are into the agreement."
"The payment terms are in the annex."
"The word Goods is as the products that are delivered."
"The old letter of intent is by this agreement."
"Any later change is accepted when both parties sign."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?She confirms that all the schedules are incorporated and the price annex is attached.
?The final draft of the service agreement is sent to Simone for review.
?Both parties sign, and the contract is executed.
?She checks the definitions and sees that the Goods are defined as the products delivered.
?She notes that the old letter of intent is superseded by the new agreement.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

The agreement is signed by both parties.
O contrato é assinado por ambas as partes.
The schedules are incorporated into the agreement.
Os anexos são incorporados ao contrato.
The old letter of intent is superseded by this agreement.
A antiga carta de intenções é substituída por este contrato.
The contract is executed when it is signed.
O contrato é formalizado quando é assinado.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the correct sentence for each situation.

You want to say the agreement is signed by both parties. What do you say?
A "The agreement signed by both parties."
B "The agreement is signed by both parties."
C "The agreement is sign by both parties."
You want to say the schedules are incorporated into the agreement. How do you say it?
A "The schedules are incorporated into the agreement."
B "The schedules are incorporate into the agreement."
C "The schedules incorporated into the agreement."
You want to say the old letter of intent is superseded by this agreement. How do you say it?
A "The old letter of intent superseded by this agreement."
B "The old letter of intent is supersede by this agreement."
C "The old letter of intent is superseded by this agreement."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are reviewing the final draft of a contract at work. Record yourself explaining four points in the passive voice: how the agreement is signed, how the schedules are incorporated, how the old letter of intent is superseded, and how each term is construed.

Survival Card -- Lesson 15

1The agreement is signed by both parties.O contrato é assinado por ambas as partes.
2The schedules are incorporated into the agreement.Os anexos são incorporados ao contrato.
3The old letter of intent is superseded by this agreement.A antiga carta de intenções é substituída por este contrato.
4The word Goods is construed as the products delivered.A palavra Goods é interpretada como os produtos entregues.
5The contract is executed when it is signed.O contrato é formalizado quando é assinado.
Aula 16 -- Pre-class

Closing the Deal -- Signing & Reported Speech

Learn the language of signing and completing a deal, and how to report what people said in a meeting: the partner said the price was final, Daniel told me the deal would close on Friday. New vocabulary: closing, completion, finalize, countersign, effective date, ratify, witness, proceeds. Grammar: reported speech -- said / told + the verb one step back.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Closing -- the final stage where a deal is signed and completed
"The closing is scheduled for Friday." (Fechamento)
Completion -- the point when a deal or transaction is fully finished
"Completion takes place on the effective date." (Conclusão)
Finalize -- to complete the terms so nothing more can change
"We finalize the price before the closing." (Finalizar)
Countersign -- to add a second signature that confirms the first
"Each party countersigns the last page." (Referendar / contra-assinar)
Effective date -- the date on which a contract starts to apply
"The effective date is the first of next month." (Data de vigência)
Ratify -- to formally approve and confirm an agreement
"The board ratifies the deal next week." (Ratificar)
Witness -- a person who watches a signing and confirms it took place
"Two witnesses are present at the signing." (Testemunha)
Proceeds -- the money received from a sale or a deal
"The proceeds are transferred the same day." (Valores / receita da operação)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Closing
Completion
Finalize
Countersign
Effective date
Ratify
Witness
Proceeds

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Daniel called Simone before the closing. The partner said the price was final, and he told Simone the board would ratify the deal that day. The client said she was happy with the terms and told Simone she would attend the signing in person. Daniel said the effective date was the first of next month, so completion takes place then. At the signing, two witnesses are present, each party countersigns the last page, and after the closing the proceeds are transferred the same day.

1. What did the partner say about the price?
A He said the price was final.
B He said the price is negotiable.
C He told the price to the client.
2. What did the client tell Simone?
A She told Simone she will not attend.
B She told Simone she would attend the signing in person.
C She said Simone the terms were wrong.
3. What happens to the proceeds after the closing?
A They are kept for one month.
B They are transferred the same day.
C They are returned to the bank.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Reported Speech

GRAMMAR

Para reportar o que alguém disse, use said ou told, e mova o verbo um passo para trás: is → was, will → would, can → could. Use told + a pessoa ("told me", "told her") e said sem pessoa ("said that..."). Nunca diga "said me". / To report what someone said, use said or told, and move the verb one step back: is → was, will → would, can → could. Use told + a person ("told me") and said with no person ("said that..."). Never say "said me".

Direct / DiretoChange / MudançaReported / Reportado
"The price is final."is → wasHe said the price was final.
"We will sign Friday."will → wouldHe told me they would sign Friday.
"I can attend."can → couldShe said she could attend.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"The partner the price was final."
"Daniel me the deal would close on Friday."
"The client said she attend the signing."
"The contract starts to apply on the ."
"Each party the last page."
"After the closing, the are transferred the same day."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?On Friday, both teams sign and two witnesses countersign the last page.
?Daniel said the agreement was ready and that the price was final.
?Finally, the proceeds are transferred the same day.
?The client said she would attend the signing in person.
?After the signing, the closing is complete and the effective date starts the deal.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

The partner said the price was final.
O sócio disse que o preço era final.
Daniel told me the deal would close on Friday.
O Daniel me disse que o negócio fecharia na sexta.
Each party countersigns the last page.
Cada parte referenda a última página.
After the closing, the proceeds are transferred.
Após o fechamento, os valores são transferidos.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the correct sentence for each situation.

The partner says: "The price is final." How do you report it?
A "The partner said the price is final."
B "The partner said the price was final."
C "The partner told the price was final."
Daniel says to you: "The deal will close on Friday." How do you report it?
A "Daniel told me the deal would close on Friday."
B "Daniel said me the deal would close on Friday."
C "Daniel told me the deal will close on Friday."
The client says: "I am happy with the terms." How do you report it?
A "The client said she is happy with the terms."
B "The client told she was happy with the terms."
C "The client said she was happy with the terms."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are briefing your team after a closing call. Record yourself reporting four things in reported speech: what the partner said about the price, what Daniel told you about the effective date, what the client said about attending, and what happens to the proceeds after the closing.

Survival Card -- Lesson 16

1The partner said the price was final.O sócio disse que o preço era final.
2Daniel told me the deal would close on Friday.O Daniel me disse que o negócio fecharia na sexta.
3The effective date is the first of next month.A data de vigência é o primeiro dia do próximo mês.
4Each party countersigns the last page.Cada parte referenda a última página.
5After the closing, the proceeds are transferred.Após o fechamento, os valores são transferidos.
Aula 17 -- Pre-class

Leading a Meeting -- Chairing & Verb Patterns

Learn the language of chairing a meeting: open it, manage the agenda and the speakers, and close it. New vocabulary: facilitate, objective, allocate, attendee, apologies, proceed, summarize, adjourn. Grammar: verb patterns -- verb + -ing (I suggest moving on) vs verb + to (I propose to vote).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Facilitate -- to guide a meeting so it runs smoothly and stays on track
"I will facilitate today's meeting." (Conduzir / facilitar)
Objective -- the clear goal a meeting is meant to reach
"The objective today is to approve the budget." (Objetivo)
Allocate -- to give a set amount of time or resources to something
"Let's allocate ten minutes to each topic." (Alocar / reservar)
Attendee -- a person who takes part in a meeting
"Every attendee will give a short update." (Participante)
Apologies -- a note of the people who could not come to the meeting
"We have apologies from two attendees today." (Ju­stificativas de ausência)
Proceed -- to move forward to the next item or stage
"Let's proceed to the next item." (Prosseguir)
Summarize -- to give a short version of the main points
"Let me summarize the decisions we made." (Resumir)
Adjourn -- to formally close or pause a meeting
"If there is nothing else, we will adjourn." (Encerrar / suspender)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Facilitate
Objective
Allocate
Attendee
Apologies
Proceed
Summarize
Adjourn

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Simone chairs the project meeting. She opens it and states the objective. She has apologies from two attendees. To keep the meeting on time, she suggests allocating ten minutes to each item, and she recommends avoiding going over time. The partner proposes to vote on the plan at the end, and he needs to allocate more budget to phase two. After everyone speaks, the attendees agree to proceed with the plan. Finally, Simone summarizes the decisions and adjourns the meeting.

1. What does Simone suggest to keep the meeting on time?
A She suggests allocating ten minutes to each item.
B She suggests to cancel the meeting.
C She suggests skip the agenda.
2. What does the partner propose?
A He proposes voting at the start.
B He proposes to vote on the plan at the end.
C He proposes to cancel the budget.
3. How does Simone close the meeting?
A She leaves without a word.
B She summarizes the decisions and adjourns the meeting.
C She asks the team to start again.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Verb Patterns

GRAMMAR

Alguns verbos são seguidos pelo verbo com -ing (suggest, recommend, avoid, consider) e outros por to + verbo (propose, decide, agree, offer, need). É o verbo que decide o padrão, não o significado. Diga "I suggest moving on" (não "to move") e "I propose to vote" (não "voting"). / Some verbs are followed by the -ing form (suggest, recommend, avoid, consider), and some by to + verb (propose, decide, agree, offer, need). The verb decides the pattern, not the meaning. Say "I suggest moving on" (not "to move") and "I propose to vote" (not "voting").

Verb / VerboPattern / PadrãoExample / Exemplo
suggest / recommend+ verb-ingI suggest moving on.
avoid / consider+ verb-ingLet's avoid going over time.
propose / decide+ to + verbI propose to vote now.
agree / offer / need+ to + verbWe agreed to allocate more time.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"I will today's meeting."
"The today is to approve the plan."
"I suggest on to the next item."
"I propose on the plan now."
"At the end, let me the decisions."
"If there is nothing else, we will ."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?The chair invites each attendee to speak and avoids going over time.
?The chair opens the meeting and states the objective and the apologies.
?Finally, the chair summarizes the decisions and adjourns the meeting.
?The chair suggests allocating time to each item on the agenda.
?When the team is ready, the chair proposes to vote and they agree to proceed.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

The objective today is to approve the plan.
O objetivo hoje é aprovar o plano.
I suggest moving on to the next item.
Eu sugiro passar para o próximo item.
I propose to vote on the proposal now.
Eu proponho votar a proposta agora.
If there is nothing else, we will adjourn.
Se não houver mais nada, vamos encerrar.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the correct sentence for each situation.

You want the team to go on to the next topic. What do you say?
A "I suggest to move on to the next item."
B "I suggest moving on to the next item."
C "I suggest move on to the next item."
You want the team to vote on the plan. What do you say?
A "I propose to vote on the plan now."
B "I propose voting on the plan now."
C "I propose vote on the plan now."
You want to keep the meeting on time. What do you say?
A "Let's avoid to go over time."
B "Let's avoid go over time."
C "Let's avoid going over time."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are chairing a short project meeting. Record yourself: open the meeting with the objective and the apologies, suggest allocating time to each item, propose to vote on the plan, and then summarize the decisions and adjourn. Use at least four new words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 17

1The objective today is to approve the plan.O objetivo hoje é aprovar o plano.
2I suggest moving on to the next item.Eu sugiro passar para o próximo item.
3I propose to vote on the proposal now.Eu proponho votar a proposta agora.
4Let me summarize the decisions before we close.Deixe-me resumir as decisões antes de encerrarmos.
5If there is nothing else, we will adjourn.Se não houver mais nada, vamos encerrar.
Aula 18 -- Pre-class

Negotiating Terms -- Bargaining & the Second Conditional

Learn the language of negotiating a deal: make a counteroffer, ask for a concession, propose a trade-off and close a mutual deal. New vocabulary: counteroffer, concession, leverage, bargain, trade-off, deadlock, flexible, mutual. Grammar: the second conditional -- if + past simple, would + verb (If we lowered the price, we would close today).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Counteroffer -- a different offer you make in reply to the other side's offer
"They offered ten, so we made a counteroffer of eight." (Contraproposta)
Concession -- something you give up so both sides can reach a deal
"We made one concession on the deadline." (Concessão)
Leverage -- the power or advantage that helps you get a better deal
"Our fast delivery gives us leverage in the talks." (Vantagem / poder de barganha)
Bargain -- to discuss terms back and forth until you agree on a price
"They like to bargain before they sign anything." (Negociar / pechinchar)
Trade-off -- a balance where you give up one thing to gain another
"A lower price for a longer contract is a fair trade-off." (Compensação / troca)
Deadlock -- a point where neither side will move and the talks are stuck
"We reached a deadlock on price, so we changed the topic." (Impasse)
Flexible -- willing to change or adapt your terms to reach a deal
"We can be flexible on the payment dates." (Flexível)
Mutual -- shared by both sides; good for everyone involved
"We found a mutual solution that worked for both companies." (Mútuo / recíproco)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

Counteroffer
Concession
Leverage
Bargain
Trade-off
Deadlock
Flexible
Mutual

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Simone negotiates the supply deal. The other side wants a discount, but the price is the bottom line. Simone makes a counteroffer: if they signed a longer contract, she would lower the price. A longer contract is a big concession, so the partner asks Simone to be flexible on the payment dates. That is a fair trade-off. Simone's leverage is fast delivery: if they accepted the price, she would deliver two weeks early. They were close to a deadlock, but in the end they find a mutual solution and close the deal.

1. What counteroffer does Simone make?
A If they signed a longer contract, she would lower the price.
B She would cancel the deal.
C She would raise the price.
2. What does the partner ask for in return?
A A higher price.
B That Simone be flexible on the payment dates.
C That Simone leave the table.
3. How do they close the deal?
A They stay in a deadlock.
B They find a mutual solution that works for both sides.
C They walk away with no deal.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Second Conditional

GRAMMAR

O second conditional fala de uma situação imaginária ou pouco provável. Estrutura: If + passado simples, ... would + verbo base. Diga "If we lowered the price, we would close today" (não "if we would lower" nem "we would to close"). Com o verbo to be, use were para todas as pessoas: "If I were you...". É diferente do first conditional (Aula 14: if + presente, will), que fala de algo real e provável. / The second conditional talks about an imaginary or unlikely situation. Structure: If + past simple, ... would + base verb. Say "If we lowered the price, we would close today" (not "if we would lower" or "we would to close"). With to be, use were for all persons: "If I were you...". It is different from the first conditional (if + present, will), which talks about something real and likely.

If-part / Parte com ifResult / ResultadoExample / Exemplo
If + past simplewould + verbIf we lowered the price, we would close.
If + past simplewould + verbIf they asked, we would offer a discount.
would + verb (first)if + past simpleWe would agree if they moved on price.
If + were (all persons)would + verbIf I were you, I would make a counteroffer.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"They offered ten, so we made a of eight."
"If we the price, we would close the deal today."
"If they asked for a discount, we a longer contract."
"A lower price for a longer contract is a fair ."
"We can be on the payment dates."
"In the end, we found a solution for both sides."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?Simone makes a counteroffer and asks for a concession in return.
?Both sides state what they want, and Simone explains the bottom line.
?Finally, both sides agree on flexible terms and close the deal.
?The other side makes an offer, and Simone studies it.
?If they reach a deadlock, they move to a different point for a mutual solution.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

That is our bottom line, but I have a counteroffer.
Esse é o nosso limite, mas eu tenho uma contraproposta.
If you signed a longer contract, we would lower the price.
Se você assinasse um contrato mais longo, nós baixaríamos o preço.
That is a fair trade-off for both of us.
Isso é uma troca justa para nós dois.
This is a mutual deal: good for both sides.
Este é um acordo mútuo: bom para os dois lados.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the correct sentence for each situation.

They ask for a discount. You want a longer contract in return. What do you say?
A "If you would sign a longer contract, we would lower the price."
B "If you signed a longer contract, we would lower the price."
C "If you signed a longer contract, we will lower the price."
You give your honest advice to a colleague. What do you say?
A "If I were you, I would make a counteroffer."
B "If I was you, I would to make a counteroffer."
C "If I am you, I would make a counteroffer."
The talks are completely stuck on price. What do you say?
A "We have a mutual on price."
B "We are at a deadlock on price."
C "We have a leverage on price."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are negotiating the terms of a deal. Record yourself: state your bottom line, make a counteroffer using the second conditional (if they did X, you would do Y), ask for a concession in return, propose a trade-off, and close with a mutual deal. Use at least four new words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 18

1That is our bottom line, but I have a counteroffer.Esse é o nosso limite, mas eu tenho uma contraproposta.
2If you signed a longer contract, we would lower the price.Se você assinasse um contrato mais longo, nós baixaríamos o preço.
3That is a fair trade-off for both of us.Isso é uma troca justa para nós dois.
4We can be flexible if you move on the deadline.Podemos ser flexíveis se você ceder no prazo.
5This is a mutual deal: good for both sides.Este é um acordo mútuo: bom para os dois lados.
Aula 19 -- Pre-class

Handling Objections -- Acknowledging Concerns & Concession Connectors

Learn how to handle an objection: acknowledge the concern, then answer with a concession connector. New vocabulary: objection, acknowledge, valid, reassure, reluctant, hesitation, overcome, willing. Grammar: concession connectors -- however, nevertheless, on the other hand (The price is high. However, our quality is much better).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Objection -- a reason or concern the other side raises against your offer
"The client raised one objection about the price." (Objeção)
Acknowledge -- to show you heard and understand the other side's concern
"I always acknowledge the concern before I answer it." (Reconhecer)
Valid -- fair and reasonable; based on good logic
"That is a valid point, and I understand your worry." (Válido / procedente)
Reassure -- to make someone feel calm and confident again
"Let me reassure you: our delivery record is excellent." (Tranquilizar / dar segurança)
Reluctant -- not yet wanting to do or agree to something
"The client was reluctant to sign a long contract." (Relutante / hesitante)
Hesitation -- a short pause that shows you are not sure or feel doubt
"After a short hesitation, the client agreed to talk." (Hesitação / receio)
Overcome -- to deal with a problem successfully and get past it
"We overcame the objection and signed the deal." (Superar)
Willing -- ready and happy to do something or to help
"We are willing to find a solution that works for you." (Disposto / disposta)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

Objection
Acknowledge
Valid
Reassure
Reluctant
Hesitation
Overcome
Willing

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

On the client call, the client raises an objection: the price is too high. Simone does not argue. First, she acknowledges the concern and says it is a valid point. Then she adds her answer with a connector: "The price is high. However, our quality is much better." The client is also reluctant to sign a long contract. Simone understands the hesitation and says: "It is a long contract; nevertheless, it protects you from price changes." Finally, the client worries about delivery. Simone answers: "You worry about delivery. On the other hand, our record is excellent," and she offers a guarantee to reassure the client. She is willing to find a solution, so together they overcome the objection.

1. What does Simone do first when the client objects?
A She acknowledges the concern and says it is a valid point.
B She argues with the client.
C She ends the call.
2. Which connector does she use to answer the price objection?
A Because.
B However.
C So.
3. How do they overcome the objection?
A The client walks away.
B Simone reassures the client and is willing to find a solution.
C Simone raises the price.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Concession Connectors

GRAMMAR

Os concession connectors (however, nevertheless, on the other hand) ligam duas ideias que contrastam: primeiro você admite a objeção, depois dá a sua resposta. São mais formais que "but". Pontuação: quando começam a segunda frase, vem depois de ponto final ou ponto-e-vírgula, e levam vírgula depois: "The price is high. However, our quality is better." Já "but" liga dentro da mesma frase, com vírgula antes: "The price is high, but the quality is better." / The concession connectors (however, nevertheless, on the other hand) join two contrasting ideas: first you admit the objection, then you give your answer. They are more formal than "but". Punctuation: when they start the second sentence, they come after a full stop or semicolon, and they take a comma after: "The price is high. However, our quality is better." "But" joins inside one sentence, with a comma before it.

ConnectorPosition / PosiçãoExample / Exemplo
However,Starts the answer; comma afterThe price is high. However, our quality is better.
Nevertheless,More formal; comma afterIt is a long contract; nevertheless, it protects you.
On the other hand,Shows the other side; comma afterYou worry about delivery. On the other hand, our record is excellent.
butJoins inside one sentence; comma beforeThe price is high, but the quality is better.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"The client raised one about the price."
"I always the concern before I answer it."
"The price is high. , our quality is much better."
"The client was to sign a long contract."
"Let me you: our record is excellent."
"We are to find a solution, so we can overcome this together."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?Simone answers with a concession connector, such as however or nevertheless.
?The client raises an objection and Simone listens carefully without interrupting.
?Finally, the hesitation is gone and together they overcome the objection.
?Simone acknowledges the concern and says it is a valid point.
?She reassures the client and shows she is willing to find a solution.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

That is a valid point, and I acknowledge your concern.
Esse é um ponto válido, e eu reconheço a sua preocupação.
The price is high. However, our quality is much better.
O preço é alto. No entanto, a nossa qualidade é muito melhor.
I understand your hesitation; nevertheless, it protects you.
Eu entendo a sua hesitação; mesmo assim, isso protege você.
We are willing to find a solution, so we can overcome this together.
Estamos dispostos a encontrar uma solução, então podemos superar isso juntos.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the correct sentence for each situation.

The client says the price is too high. You want to answer formally. What do you say?
A "The price is high, however our quality is better."
B "The price is high. However, our quality is better."
C "The price is high, but however our quality is better."
The client raises a concern. What should you do before you answer?
A "That is a valid point, and I acknowledge your concern."
B "You are wrong about that."
C "Let us not talk about it."
The client is not sure and pauses. How do you describe that?
A "The client made an objection of price."
B "The client had a hesitation and was reluctant."
C "The client was willing to overcome."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine a client raises an objection: your price is too high. Record yourself handling it: acknowledge the concern, say it is a valid point, answer with a concession connector (however or nevertheless), reassure the client, and show you are willing to find a solution. Use at least four new words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 19

1That is a valid point, and I acknowledge your concern.Esse é um ponto válido, e eu reconheço a sua preocupação.
2The price is high. However, our quality is much better.O preço é alto. No entanto, a nossa qualidade é muito melhor.
3I understand your hesitation; nevertheless, it protects you.Eu entendo a sua hesitação; mesmo assim, isso protege você.
4Let me reassure you: our record is excellent.Deixe-me tranquilizá-lo: o nosso histórico é excelente.
5We are willing to find a solution, so we can overcome this together.Estamos dispostos a encontrar uma solução, então podemos superar isso juntos.
Aula 20 -- Pre-class

Social English -- Conversation at the Corporate Dinner

Learn the social English for a corporate dinner: how to mingle, give a compliment, recommend a dish, join a toast and leave the table politely. New vocabulary: host, mingle, compliment, cuisine, recommend, toast, cheers, excuse me. Grammar: the present perfect for experience -- have/has + past participle (Have you ever tried this cuisine? I have been to Tokyo twice).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Host -- the person who invites you and welcomes you to the event
"The host greeted every guest at the door." (Anfitrião / anfitriã)
Mingle -- to move around and talk to many different people at a social event
"Before dinner, the guests mingle and make small talk." (Circular / interagir)
Compliment -- a nice thing you say to please someone
"She gave the host a compliment about the meal." (Elogio)
Cuisine -- the style of cooking of a country or region
"Have you ever tried Japanese cuisine?" (Culinária / gastronomia)
Recommend -- to say that something is good and that someone should try it
"The host recommended the fish; it was excellent." (Recomendar)
Toast -- when people lift a glass and say words to celebrate together
"The host made a short toast to a successful deal." (Brinde)
Cheers -- what you say when you touch glasses to celebrate
"They lifted their glasses and said cheers." (Saúde! / Tim-tim!)
Excuse me -- a polite phrase to leave the table, pass by, or get attention
"Excuse me for a moment; I will be right back." (Com licença)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

Host
Mingle
Compliment
Cuisine
Recommend
Toast
Cheers
Excuse me

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

After the deal, there is a corporate dinner. The host welcomes everyone at the door, and the guests mingle before they sit down. Simone meets a guest and starts with light social talk, not business. She gives a compliment: "The cuisine looks wonderful." Then she asks about experience with the present perfect: "Have you ever tried this kind of food?" The guest says, "Yes, I have tried it once," and he recommends the fish. Later the host gives a toast, everyone lifts a glass and says cheers. When Simone needs to leave the table, she says "Excuse me for a moment" and goes to thank the host for a lovely evening.

1. What do the guests do before they sit down?
A They mingle and make light social talk.
B They sign the contract.
C They leave the restaurant.
2. Which question does Simone use to ask about experience?
A "Did you try this food yesterday?"
B "Have you ever tried this kind of food?"
C "Are you trying this food now?"
3. What happens during the toast?
A Everyone leaves the table.
B Everyone lifts a glass and says cheers.
C The host signs the deal.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect

GRAMMAR

Usamos o present perfect (have / has + particípio passado) para falar de experiência na vida, quando o tempo exato não importa. Em perguntas usamos ever (alguma vez): "Have you ever tried this cuisine?". Em negativas usamos never: "I have never visited this city." Particípios: be → been, eat → eaten, meet → met; verbos regulares levam -ed (try → tried, visit → visited). Cuidado: depois de have/has vem o particípio, não o verbo base (tried, não try). / We use the present perfect (have/has + past participle) for experience in your life, when the exact time is not important. Use ever in questions and never in negatives. After have/has, use the past participle, not the base verb.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
QuestionHave you ever + participle?Have you ever tried this cuisine?
AffirmativeI/you/we have + participleI have been to Tokyo twice.
He/She/Ithas + participleShe has met the host before.
Negativehave/has never + participleI have never visited this city.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"The welcomed every guest at the door."
"Have you ever this cuisine?"
"I have to this restaurant before; I recommend the fish."
"She gave a nice about the wonderful cuisine."
"The host gave a and everyone said cheers."
" me for a moment; I will be right back."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?She asks about experience: have you been here before, have you ever tried this cuisine.
?Simone arrives at the corporate dinner and the host welcomes her at the door.
?Finally, she says excuse me politely and thanks the host for a lovely evening.
?She mingles with the other guests and starts with light social topics, not business.
?The host gives a toast, everyone lifts a glass, and they all say cheers.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Have you ever tried this cuisine?
Você já experimentou essa culinária alguma vez?
The food is wonderful. Thank you for a lovely evening.
A comida está maravilhosa. Obrigada por uma noite encantadora.
I have been to this restaurant before; I recommend the fish.
Eu já estive neste restaurante antes; eu recomendo o peixe.
Excuse me for a moment; I will be right back.
Com licença por um momento; eu já volto.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the correct sentence for each situation.

You want to ask a guest about their experience with the food. What do you say?
A "Have you ever try this cuisine?"
B "Have you ever tried this cuisine?"
C "Do you ever tried this cuisine?"
The host raises a glass for a toast. What do you say?
A "Cheers, everyone! To a successful partnership."
B "Let us sign the contract now."
C "I have an objection."
You need to leave the table for a moment. What do you say?
A "Go away, please."
B "Excuse me for a moment; I will be right back."
C "I am leaving now, bye."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are at a corporate dinner and you sit next to a guest you have never met. Record yourself making conversation: give a compliment about the cuisine, ask two questions about experience with the present perfect (Have you ever...?), and join the toast with cheers. Use at least four new words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 20

1Have you ever tried this cuisine?Você já experimentou essa culinária alguma vez?
2The food is wonderful. Thank you for a lovely evening.A comida está maravilhosa. Obrigada por uma noite encantadora.
3I have been to this restaurant before; I recommend the fish.Eu já estive neste restaurante antes; eu recomendo o peixe.
4Cheers, everyone! To a successful partnership.Saúde, pessoal! A uma parceria de sucesso.
5Excuse me for a moment; I will be right back.Com licença por um momento; eu já volto.

IN CLASS -- Select Lesson

01
Who Is Simone?
Diagnostic + Self-Introduction -- 35 slides
02
My Daily Routine
Present Simple + Adverbs of Frequency -- 35 slides
03
Email Essentials
Write Clear Corporate Emails -- Opening, Body & Closing -- 27 slides
04
Phone & Video Calls
Open, Clarify & Close a Professional Call -- 27 slides
05
Small Talk That Works
Start, Build & Keep a Professional Conversation -- 27 slides
06
Meeting Basics
Take Part in a Meeting with Can, Could & Should -- 27 slides
07
Expressing Opinions
Share and Defend Your View with I Think & In My View -- 27 slides
08
Giving Updates
Report Progress with the Present Continuous & Time Expressions -- 27 slides
09
Asking Smart Questions
Get the Information You Need with Question Words & Indirect Questions -- 27 slides
10
Travel English I
Travel Vocabulary & Polite Requests with Could I / Could you -- 27 slides
11
Contract Vocabulary
